mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal without requiring threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 347665M as of 2005-01-01 reflects robust consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS growth periods produce negative average forward returns in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5%, monetary tightening reduces equity valuations by raising the discount rate applied to future cash flows. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when activated.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.28% as of 2005-01-01 is near but below tightening threshold
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 2.5%, forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward returns is positive and exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating payroll employment (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions and rising corporate profitability, generating positive equity returns. The mechanism requires joint observation of PAYEMS growth and declining unemployment (UNRATE) to confirm demand-driven labor strength rather than cyclical noise.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 132573K and UNRATE at 5.2% as of 2005-01-01
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Interaction signal (PAYEMS growth + UNRATE decline) produces positive 60-day forward returns in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or the joint signal's Rank IC is not significantly higher than either proxy alone (difference < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines signal weakening supply-side capacity and potential stagflation risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings and inflation expectations.
failure_condition: When INDPRO declines > 0.5% MoM, 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO changes and 5-day returns is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal broad-based demand strength across residential and consumer sectors. When both rise simultaneously, equity valuations expand due to improved earnings visibility and reduced recession risk over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2004K and RSAFS at 347665M as of 2005-01-01
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across housing and consumption
failure_condition: Joint signal (HOUST + RSAFS growth) produces positive 60-day forward returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or interaction effect (joint signal IC minus max single-proxy IC) is negative or less than 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerating above 2.5% year-over-year forces the Federal Reserve to maintain or raise rates, compressing equity risk premiums and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: inflationary pressure regime requiring monetary response
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL 12-month growth > 2.5%, 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC between CPI growth and forward returns is positive and significant (> 0.04).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 5.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.2% as of 2005-01-01, currently below trigger threshold
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 5.5%, 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between UNRATE level and forward returns is negative or below 0.02 in absolute value.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating below 2% annualized signals late-cycle weakness and earnings recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors reduce equity allocations in anticipation of profit margin compression.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 10975.7B (vintage) as of 2004-10-01 reflects Q3 2004 demand
failure_condition: When GDPC1 quarterly growth < 2% annualized, 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC between GDP growth and forward returns is positive and exceeds 0.03.
